text: My name is Phuc Tran, also known as Patrick Tran. I am an iOS and full-stack software engineer with over 4 years of experience, based in Sacramento, California.

I work as an iOS and full-stack engineer. I build and ship mobile applications in Swift and SwiftUI, as well as production web and backend systems using React, TypeScript, and Python with FastAPI. I also integrate AI-driven features including computer vision and machine learning models.

Patrick Tran has delivered apps from concept to launch, including an independent app published on the App Store and AI-powered solutions for healthcare and consumer applications. He has 4+ years of professional experience across mobile, web, and backend development.

I am pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science at California State University, Sacramento, with an expected graduation date of May 2026. I have one class remaining in the spring semester and hold a GPA of 3.2.
My relevant coursework includes Data Structures and Algorithms, Object-Oriented Design, Database Management, Software Engineering, and Artificial Intelligence.
My mobile development skills include Swift, SwiftUI, and UIKit. I have built and shipped iOS applications to the App Store, including modular SwiftUI component architectures and UIKit integrations.
My web and frontend skills include TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS. I build responsive web interfaces and full-stack web applications.
My backend skills include Python, FastAPI, REST API design, SQL, Node.js, and Express. I am experienced with asynchronous programming and building scalable API systems.
I have experience with databases including Supabase (PostgreSQL), Firebase (NoSQL), and vector databases for AI retrieval systems.

My AI and machine learning skills include OpenCV, MediaPipe, TensorFlow, PyTorch, sentence-transformers, HuggingFace Transformers, and working with large language models such as OpenAI GPT and Anthropic Claude.
My tools and platforms include Git, Firebase Remote Config and Analytics, AWS Amplify, Docker, Raspberry Pi, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Conda environment management, and Linux server deployment.

At NeuroSpring from February 2025 to December 2025, I worked as a Full-Stack Engineer on a hospital camera detection project. I architected and developed a real-time patient-monitoring platform to detect involuntary movement patterns for rapid ambulance deployment. I built cross-platform UIs using React and TypeScript for web and Raspberry Pi devices, deployed backend APIs with FastAPI, managed data using Supabase for real-time processing, and integrated MediaPipe along with a custom AI model called UNIK for posture and movement analysis.

At FanFly from October 2023 to December 2024, I worked as an iOS Engineer on a show ticket selling application. I led development of the company's first iOS app that connected fans and artists using modular SwiftUI components. I created reusable UI templates that reduced feature development time by about 30 percent, authored onboarding documentation, and resolved memory issues that helped boost app usage by 40 percent.

At Scoop Inc. from May 2023 to September 2023, I worked as an iOS Intern on a carpooling and commuting application. I converted Figma designs into responsive SwiftUI and UIKit interfaces, and integrated REST APIs using PromiseKit and Alamofire to support smooth asynchronous interactions.

My development style is practical and product-focused. I prefer clean modular architecture, clear naming, and building systems that are easy to extend. I care about code quality and keeping things simple.
I am interested in AI systems, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), real-time detection systems, scalable API design, iOS development, and building tools that solve real problems.
